results.description: DESCRIPTION:THIS PROJECT WILL DEVELOP NEW MODELS, METHODS, AND SOFTWARE TOOLS FOR DISCOVERING AND DESIGNING NOVEL REACTION NETWORKS AND PROCESSES THAT ARE ESSENTIAL FOR TRANSFORMING THE US CHEMICALS INDUSTRY TOWARD GREATER SUSTAINABILITY AND CIRCULARITY.ACTIVITIES:SPECIFIC ACTIVITIES INCLUDE DEVELOPING, I) AN OPEN-ACCESS MODEL OF THE CURRENT US CHEMICALS INDUSTRY, II) INNOVATIVE METHODS FOR DISCOVERING NOVEL CIRCULAR CHEMICAL REACTION NETWORKS, III) APPROACHES FOR ENGAGING WITH LOCAL COMMUNITIES AND QUANTIFYING  IMPACTS OF CURRENT AND PROSPECTIVE CHEMICAL INDUSTRIES AT SPECIFIC LOCATIONS, IV) A FRAMEWORK FOR EFFICIENTLY GUIDING THE SELECTION AND DESIGN OF BETTER REACTION NETWORKS AND PROCESSES, AND V) APPLICATIONS TO INDUSTRIALLY RELEVANT PRODUCTS SUCH AS METHANOL AND POLYETHYLENE AND USER-FRIENDLY SOFTWARE TOOLS.SUBRECIPIENT:THE SUBAWARD TO UNLIMITED POTENTIAL ($100,000) IS TO DEVELOP AND IMPLEMENT EDUCATIONAL PRESENTATIONS, FOCUS GROUPS, AND TRAIN COMMUNITY LEADERS ON ENVIRONMENTAL EDUCATION.OUTCOMES:ANTICIPATED DELIVERABLES AND EXPECTED OUTCOMES OF THIS WORK INCLUDE A MODEL OF THE U.S. CHEMICALS PRODUCTION AND MANUFACTURING INDUSTRY, NOVEL METHODOLOGY AND FRAMEWORK FOR DEVELOPING REACTION NETWORKS, HIERARCHICAL SCREENING PROCESSES, AND USER-FRIENDLY SOFTWARE TOOLS THAT PROMOTE SUSTAINABILITY AND CIRCULARITY. INTENDED BENEFICIARIES INCLUDE THE CHEMICALS MANUFACTURING INDUSTRY, COMMUNITIES AFFECTED BY ENVIRONMENTAL EXPOSURE TO TOXIC CHEMICAL PRODUCTS, THE GENERAL PUBLIC, AND ENVIRONMENTAL HEALTH DECISION-MAKERS.
